title: Insulin-Induced Hypoglycemia
chief_complaint: My husband is confused, sweating, and shaking. He's diabetic. He took his insulin this morning but didn't eat breakfast.

physical_exam.Neurologic: Confused, unable to follow commands. No focal deficits.

hidden_data.insulin: Insulin level 45 µU/mL (high - exogenous insulin). C-peptide 0.2 ng/mL (low - suppressed endogenous insulin)

key_learning_points: Whipple triad: Symptoms of hypoglycemia + low glucose + resolution of symptoms with glucose administration
Severe hypoglycemia = glucose <50 mg/dL + altered mental status requiring assistance
Treatment: D50W 1 amp (25g) IV if IV access. Glucagon 1mg IM if no IV access. Recheck glucose in 15 minutes
High insulin + low C-peptide = exogenous insulin (factitious or accidental). High insulin + high C-peptide = insulinoma
Prevention: Education about insulin timing with meals, sick day rules, and hypoglycemia recognition
